# Seoul city guide

## Overview

Seoul (서울) is a city where 600-year-old palaces sit next to neon-lit skyscrapers. It's fast, loud, delicious, and endlessly surprising. The subway system is world-class, street food is everywhere, and you can go from a tranquil temple to a rooftop bar in 15 minutes.

**Best time to visit**: Spring (Apr-May) for cherry blossoms, Fall (Sep-Oct) for foliage.
**Currency**: Korean Won (KRW). 1,300 KRW ≈ 1 USD.
**Language tip**: English is common in tourist areas but learning basic Korean goes a long way.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Myeongdong (명동) | Busy, commercial | Shopping, skincare, street food | Line 4, Myeongdong Station |
| Hongdae (홍대) | Young, artsy, loud | Nightlife, indie shops, street performances | Line 2, Hongik Univ Station |
| Ikseon-dong (익선동) | Trendy, hanok charm | Cafes, photography, boutique shops | Line 3, Jongno 3-ga Station |
| Gangnam (강남) | Sleek, upscale | Fine dining, K-pop culture, COEX Mall | Line 2, Gangnam Station |
| Bukchon (북촌) | Traditional, scenic | Hanok village walks, cultural sites | Line 3, Anguk Station |
| Itaewon (이태원) | International, diverse | Global food, vintage shops, LGBTQ+ friendly | Line 6, Itaewon Station |
| Jongno (종로) | Historic, central | Palaces, temples, traditional markets | Line 1, Jongno Station |
| Yeouido (여의도) | Business, riverside | Cherry blossoms (spring), riverside walks | Line 5, Yeouido Station |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gwangjang Market (광장마켓) | Street food | Jongno | $ | The grandmothers here have been making bindaetteok (mung bean pancakes) for decades. The energy is electric. | Bindaetteok, mayak gimbap, yukhoe (raw beef) |
| Tosokchon Samgyetang (토속촌) | Korean traditional | Jongno | $$ | The line is worth it. Ginseng chicken soup that warms your soul. | Samgyetang (ginseng chicken soup) |
| Maple Tree House (단풍나무집) | Korean BBQ | Itaewon | $$$ | Premium galbi in a gorgeous setting. This is the BBQ experience. | Yangnyeom galbi (marinated short ribs) |
| Tongin Market (통인시장) | Street food | Jongno | $ | Buy brass coins, fill your tray from different stalls. It's a lunch adventure. | Tteokbokki, japchae, fried items |
| Cafe Onion (카페어니언) | Cafe | Ikseon-dong | $$ | A converted hanok turned into the most photogenic cafe in Seoul. The pandoro bread is iconic. | Pandoro, cream latte |
| Myeongdong Kyoja (명동교자) | Noodles | Myeongdong | $ | Cash only, no-frills, legendary kalguksu. The dumplings are enormous. | Kalguksu (knife-cut noodles), mandu |
| Line Friends Store Cafe | Cafe/themed | Gangnam | $$ | For the K-pop and character culture experience. Fun, not serious dining. | Character-themed drinks |
| Noryangjin Fish Market (노량진) | Seafood | Yeongdeungpo | $$ | Pick your fish alive, they prepare it upstairs. Freshest sashimi in the city. | Live sashimi, spicy seafood stew |
| Jungsik (정식) | Fine dining | Gangnam | $$$ | Modern Korean tasting menu. Michelin-starred. A special occasion place. | Tasting menu |
| Isaac Toast | Breakfast | Multiple | $ | Korea's famous egg toast chain. Sweet, savory, perfect hangover food. | Ham cheese egg toast |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gyeongbokgung Palace (경복궁) | Culture | Jongno | $ | 2-3 hours | The grandest of Seoul's five palaces. Rent a hanbok for free entry and amazing photos. |
| N Seoul Tower (남산타워) | Views | Namsan | $$ | 1-2 hours | Take the cable car up for sunset views over the entire city. The love locks are iconic. |
| Bukchon Hanok Village (북촌한옥마을) | Culture | Bukchon | Free | 1-2 hours | Wander through traditional Korean houses on hilly streets. Quiet mornings are best. |
| COEX Starfield Library | Culture | Gangnam | Free | 1 hour | A massive open library inside a mall. The bookshelves are two stories tall. Instagram heaven. |
| Changdeokgung Secret Garden (창덕궁) | Nature/Culture | Jongno | $$ | 2 hours | A UNESCO World Heritage palace with a hidden garden. Guided tours only. Book ahead. |
| Namsan Cable Car | Scenic | Namsan | $$ | 30 min | Ride up to N Seoul Tower. Better than hiking if you're short on time. |
| Cheonggyecheon Stream (청계천) | Walk | Central | Free | 1 hour | A restored urban stream running through downtown. Beautiful at night with lights. |
| Lotte World Tower Observatory | Views | Songpa | $$$ | 1-2 hours | 555 meters high, glass floor sky deck. For the brave ones. |
| War Memorial of Korea | Museum | Itaewon | Free | 2-3 hours | Powerful, well-done museum. The outdoor exhibits are impressive. |
| Dongdaemun Design Plaza (DDP) | Architecture | Dongdaemun | Free | 1 hour | Zaha Hadid's alien spaceship building. Night markets outside on weekends. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hongdae Club Street | Clubs/bars | Hongdae | $-$$ | The epicenter of Seoul nightlife. Live music, dance clubs, street performers until dawn. |
| Itaewon Pub Mile | Bars | Itaewon | $$ | International crowd, craft cocktails, rooftop bars. More chill than Hongdae. |
| Pojangmacha (포장마차) | Street tents | Multiple | $ | Plastic tent street bars serving soju and fried chicken. The real Seoul experience. |
| Soap Seoul | Club | Itaewon | $$ | Techno and house music. Seoul's answer to Berlin clubs. |
| Seoul Forest Wine Bar | Wine | Seongsu | $$$ | Trendy natural wine spot in Seoul's "Brooklyn" neighborhood. |

## Transit tips

- **T-money card**: Buy one at any convenience store. Works on subway, buses, and taxis.
- **Subway**: Clean, fast, English signage everywhere. Runs 5:30 AM to midnight.
- **Buses**: Excellent network but confusing for first-timers. Subway is easier.
- **Taxis**: Cheap compared to Western cities. Regular (silver/white) is fine.
- **KTX**: High-speed train to Busan (2.5 hours) or other cities. Book at Korail.
- **Pro tip**: Download Naver Map or KakaoMap. Google Maps is unreliable in Korea.

## Useful Korean phrases

| English | Korean | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | 안녕하세요 | annyeonghaseyo |
| Thank you | 감사합니다 | gamsahamnida |
| Delicious! | 맛있어요! | mashisseoyo! |
| How much is this? | 이거 얼마예요? | igeo eolmayeyo? |
| One more please | 하나 더 주세요 | hana deo juseyo |
| Cheers! | 건배! | geonbae! |
| Where is...? | ...어디예요? | ...eodiyeyo? |
| The bill please | 계산해 주세요 | gyesanhae juseyo |
